helpful: Provisioned full petition text made the split/vehicle analysis possible without any document fetching.
Per-Term paid-vs-IFP grant rates in statpack.json were the single most useful quantitative anchor for a paid petition with no relist/CVSG signal yet.
gaps: Corpus query sidecar was unreachable (fedcourts query timed out), so no ranked similar-petition priors were available.
statpack.md in this checkout has no 'Segment base rate by salience band' table the prompt describes, so the salience-band anchor could not be used.

CourtListener has no docket-entry data for SCOTUS dockets (companion-petition status checks are limited to filed/terminated metadata); a topic-level lookup of prior cert petitions presenting the same circuit split would have been valuable.
notes: No BIO on the docket and distribution straight to the long conference means the strongest observable signals (CFR, relists) simply have not had a chance to occur yet; a prediction cell re-run after the 2026-09-28 conference would be much better informed.
